new_classes.definition_en: Sparkling wine of fresh grapes, other than Champagne (2204 10 11), Cava (2204 10 13), Prosecco (2204 10 15), and Asti or Moscato d'Asti (2204 10 93), that bears a protected designation of origin (PDO) or protected geographical indication (PGI) and is classified under subheading 2204 10 96 of the Combined Nomenclature.

new_classes.label_en: Protected Designation of Origin or Geographical Indication quality (PDO/PGI)
new_classes.label_de: Qualität mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung oder geschützter geografischer Angabe (g.U./g.g.A.)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a wine indicating that it carries a protected designation of origin (PDO) or a protected geographical indication (PGI) under EU wine law, as required for classification under CN subheadings 2204 10 93–2204 10 96.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for 22041096 is minimal, referring only to the range 22041011–22041098 and to Subheading Note 1 of Chapter 22 (which governs the distinction between quality sparkling wines bearing a PDO/PGI and other sparkling wines). The core distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) the wine is a sparkling wine (SparklingWineQuality restriction); (2) it bears a PDO or PGI (new ProtectedDesignationOfOriginOrGeographicalIndicationQuality restriction); (3) it is none of the explicitly named PDO/PGI sparkling wines (Champagne 22041011, Prosecco 22041015). Gaps: (a) Cava (2204 10 13) and Asti/Moscato d'Asti (2204 10 93) sibling classes do not yet exist in the TBox and therefore cannot be referenced in complement restrictions — their exclusion is described in coverage_explanation only; (b) the Subheading Note 1 criteria (excess pressure ≥ 3 bar at 20°C, actual alcoholic strength by volume constraints) are already partially modelled via SparklingWineQuality in the running TBox but no numeric pressure threshold restriction is placed here because the property excessPressureBar exists but a full equivalence axiom for the sparkling-wine quality class itself is out of scope for this leaf node. Coverage is therefore partial.
